steps.title: Find the slope using the two given points
steps.content:
Apply the slope formula with $(1, 8)$ and $(7, 0)$:
$$m = \frac{8 - 0}{1 - 7} = \frac{8}{-6} = -\frac{4}{3}$$

steps.title: Find the equation and identify the y-intercept
steps.content:
Use point-slope form with $(7, 0)$ and slope $-\frac{4}{3}$:
$$y - 0 = -\frac{4}{3}(x - 7)$$
$$y = -\frac{4}{3}x + \frac{28}{3}$$
Since the y-intercept occurs when $x = 0$, we have $b = \frac{28}{3}$.
